2603.21165.title: Many Dialects, Many Languages, One Cultural Lens: Evaluating Multilingual VLMs for Bengali Culture Understanding Across Historically Linked Languages and Regional Dialects
2603.21165.paper_summary: Bangla culture is richly expressed through region, dialect, history, food, politics, media, and everyday visual life, yet it remains underrepresented in multimodal evaluation. To address this gap, we introduce BanglaVerse, a culturally grounded benchmark for evaluating multilingual vision-language models (VLMs) on Bengali culture across historically linked languages and regional dialects. Built from 1,152 manually curated images across nine domains, the benchmark supports visual question answering and captioning, and is expanded into four languages and five Bangla dialects, yielding ~32.3K artifacts. Our experiments show that evaluating only standard Bangla overestimates true model capability: performance drops under dialectal variation, especially for caption generation, while historically linked languages such as Hindi and Urdu retain some cultural meaning but remain weaker for structured reasoning. Across domains, the main bottleneck is missing cultural knowledge rather than visual grounding alone, with knowledge-intensive categories. These findings position BanglaVerse as a more realistic test bed for measuring culturally grounded multimodal understanding under linguistic variation.
2603.21165.paper_summary_zh: 孟加拉文化透過地區、方言、歷史、食物、政治、媒體和日常視覺生活豐富地表達出來，但在多模態評估中仍然未被充分代表。為了解決這一差距，我們介紹了BanglaVerse，一個以文化為基礎的基準，用於評估多語言視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在孟加拉文化上的表現，涵蓋歷史相關語言和地區方言。該基準由九個領域的1,152張手動策劃的圖像組成，支持視覺問答和標題生成，並擴展到四種語言和五種孟加拉方言，產生約32.3K的文物。我們的實驗顯示，僅評估標準孟加拉語會高估模型的真實能力：在方言變異下，性能下降，特別是在標題生成方面，而歷史相關語言如印地語和烏爾都語保留了一些文化意義，但在結構推理方面仍然較弱。在各個領域中，主要瓶頸在於缺乏文化知識，而不僅僅是視覺基礎，尤其是在知識密集型類別中。這些發現使BanglaVerse成為衡量文化根植的多模態理解在語言變異下的更現實的測試平台。
2603.21165.author: Nurul Labib Sayeedi et.al.
2603.21165.authors: Nurul Labib Sayeedi, Md. Faiyaz Abdullah Sayeedi, Shubhashis Roy Dipta, Rubaya Tabassum, Ariful Ekraj Hridoy, Mehraj Mahmood, Mahbub E Sobhani, Md. Tarek Hasan, Swakkhar Shatabda
